commonProblems.h2: Honda Thats Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Honda Thats kei car relies on the compact EN07 series engine, with timing chain tensioner wear and turbo wastegate failure dominating replacement enquiries for this JDM import. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair options and understand when replacement offers better value for your ultra-niche tall wagon.

problems.group: Honda Thats Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.h4: Honda Thats Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - Why EN07 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Honda Thats G, X, Special Edition (2002-2008, EN07V/EN07W/EN07X)
problems.rootCause: The EN07 series uses a compact timing chain design with a hydraulic tensioner prone to wear under kei car operating conditions, especially on high-mileage JDM imports with uncertain service history.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioner, guide rails, and chain inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only before chain stretch occurs; 20k-40k mile extension if caught early

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides, sprockets, and seals
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for engines over 70k miles; addresses root cause but not other wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage imports; resolves all internal wear simultaneously

problems.recommendation: On a Honda Thats worth £3,000-£6,000 as a collector kei import, preventative tensioner replacement makes sense only if the engine is otherwise healthy and under 70k miles. For higher-mileage examples or those with additional symptoms (oil consumption, noise), a rebuilt EN07 unit offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EN07 engine prices for your Honda Thats

problems.h4: Honda Thats Turbo Wastegate Failure - Why EN07W/X Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Honda Thats X, Special Edition (2002-2008, EN07W/EN07X)
problems.rootCause: The small turbocharger on EN07W/X engines uses a mechanical wastegate prone to carbon buildup and pivot wear, causing boost loss, rattling noises, and reduced performance on high-mileage JDM imports.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean linkage, adjust actuator rod, test boost pressure
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix only; suitable for early-stage rattle with no performance loss

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Rebuild with Wastegate Repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, rebuild cartridge, replace wastegate components, balance
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is healthy; requires specialist kei turbo expertise

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt turbo engine with upgraded wastegate, bench-tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage imports or when engine shows additional wear signs

problems.recommendation: For Honda Thats X or Special Edition owners noticing boost loss or wastegate rattle, a turbo rebuild is cost-effective only if compression tests confirm the engine block is healthy. Given the EN07W/X's known tensioner and oil consumption issues, many owners find a full rebuilt engine offers better peace of mind and warranty coverage for this ultra-niche JDM import.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EN07W turbo Honda Thats engine replacement

problems.h4: Honda Thats Oil Consumption Problem - Why EN07 Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Honda Thats G, X, Special Edition (2002-2008, EN07V/EN07W/EN07X)
problems.rootCause: High-mileage EN07 engines commonly develop oil consumption due to piston ring wear, valve stem seal hardening, and (on turbo variants) turbo seal wear - exacerbated by uncertain service history on JDM imports.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, valve cover gasket, inspect seals
repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses minor consumption; not viable for heavy oil burn

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace piston rings, valve seals, gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block and crank are healthy; requires specialist kei engine expertise

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded rings/seals, bench-tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage imports with heavy oil consumption or multiple symptoms

problems.recommendation: On a Honda Thats consuming over 1 litre per 1,000 miles, a top-end rebuild is rarely cost-effective given the labour intensity and risk of uncovering further wear. For collector-level JDM imports, a rebuilt EN07 engine with upgraded components and warranty typically offers better value and reliability for this ultra-niche tall wagon.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EN07 engine prices for oil consumption fix

problems.h4: Honda Thats CVT Compatibility Issue - Why Transmission Matching Matters
problems.affectedModels: Honda Thats G CVT, X CVT (2002-2008, EN07V/EN07W with CVT)
problems.rootCause: EN07 engines paired with Jatco CVT transmissions require precise torque converter and ECU matching; incorrect engine code or rebuild specification can cause shuddering, error codes, or transmission damage on JDM imports.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - CVT Fluid Service & Adaptation Reset
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain/refill CVT fluid, reset ECU adaptation values via diagnostic tool
repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses minor shudder; not viable for compatibility mismatches

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace torque converter, flush CVT system, reprogram ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine is healthy but converter is worn; requires CVT specialist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine verified for CVT compatibility, bench-tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for ensuring correct torque converter match and ECU calibration on JDM imports

problems.recommendation: For Honda Thats CVT owners, engine replacement must explicitly confirm CVT compatibility - including torque converter specification and ECU mapping. Given the rarity of JDM kei CVT expertise in the UK, sourcing a rebuilt EN07 engine from a specialist who verifies CVT match is essential to avoid costly transmission damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Honda Thats CVT-compatible engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Honda Thats
finalCta.paragraph: The Honda Thats represents an ultra-niche segment of the UK JDM import market, with EN07 series engine failures requiring specialist knowledge and parts sourcing. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists experienced in kei car rebuilds, offering rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing known tensioner, turbo, and oil consumption issues. Every unit includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery - compare prices from trusted Honda Thats specialists and keep your collector tall wagon on the road without main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Honda Thats Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Honda Thats and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

faq.h2: Honda Thats Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Honda Thats engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Honda Thats engine replacement costs typically range from £2,450-£3,150 for a rebuilt EN07 unit supply-only, with supply & fit adding £850-£1,450 depending on location and variant. Used engines start around £1,250 but carry higher risk for this ultra-niche JDM import.
keyPoints: Rebuilt EN07V (NA) from £2,450, EN07W/X (turbo) from £2,650
Supply & fit labour typically adds £850-£1,450 nationwide
Used JDM engines £1,250-£1,850 with compression report recommended
12-month warranty standard on rebuilt units through EngineMarket

items.question: What is the most reliable Honda Thats engine code?
items.answer: The EN07V naturally aspirated engine is generally considered the most reliable Honda Thats code, with fewer complex components than turbo variants, though all EN07 codes share timing chain tensioner wear concerns at high mileage.
keyPoints: EN07V (NA) has fewer failure points than turbo EN07W/X
All EN07 codes prone to tensioner wear at 60k-90k miles
Turbo variants add wastegate and oil consumption risks
Regular oil changes critical for all EN07 longevity
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable EN07V Honda Thats engine replacement

items.question: How do I find my Honda Thats engine code on a JDM import?
items.answer: Your Honda Thats engine code is stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head, typically visible after removing the engine cover; EN07V, EN07W or EN07X will be clearly marked alongside the serial number.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near cylinder head
EN07V = NA, EN07W = turbo, EN07X = VTEC turbo
DVLA lookup via registration confirms variant but not code
Photo documentation recommended when sourcing replacement
items.cta: Verify your Honda Thats engine code before ordering replacement

items.question: Can I fit a Daihatsu Mira engine in my Honda Thats?
items.answer: Yes, Honda Thats engines are fully compatible with Daihatsu Mira/Move platforms as they share the EN07 engine family, but confirm exact code match (EN07V/W/X) and transmission type before sourcing a replacement.
keyPoints: Honda Thats is rebadged Daihatsu Mira Move tall wagon
EN07V/W/X codes interchangeable between platforms
Confirm CVT vs manual transmission compatibility
Daihatsu parts availability can aid UK sourcing
items.cta: Compare Daihatsu-compatible Honda Thats engine prices

items.question: What causes Honda Thats engines to fail on JDM imports?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner wear is the most common Honda Thats engine failure, followed by turbo wastegate issues on EN07W/X and oil consumption from piston ring wear on high-mileage JDM imports with uncertain service history.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner wear at 60k-90k miles most prevalent
Turbo wastegate rattle and boost loss on EN07W/X
Oil consumption from ring/seal wear on 70k+ mile units
CVT compatibility mismatches can cause secondary damage
items.cta: Get quotes for Honda Thats engine failure repair solutions

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Honda Thats engine on a grey market import?
items.answer: For collector-level Honda Thats imports valued £3,000-£8,000, engine replacement is often worthwhile given the model's rarity and enthusiast demand, especially with a rebuilt EN07 unit carrying warranty protection.
keyPoints: Collector interest supports £3k-£8k valuations for good examples
Rebuilt EN07 engine £2,450-£3,150 plus fitting
12-month warranty reduces long-term ownership risk
Specialist knowledge required for JDM kei car rebuilds
items.cta: Compare Honda Thats engine replacement vs vehicle value

items.question: How long does a Honda Thats engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: Honda Thats engine replacement typically takes 2-4 weeks for rebuilt units sourced from UK stock, or 4-8 weeks if Japan-sourcing is required, plus 1-2 days for supply & fit installation at a specialist centre.
keyPoints: UK-stock rebuilt engines: 2-4 week lead time typical
Japan-sourced units: 4-8 weeks including shipping
Supply & fit installation: 1-2 days at specialist centre
CVT models may require additional ECU calibration time

items.answer: Yes, rebuilt and reconditioned Honda Thats engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, with extended coverage options available for collector-grade EN07 rebuilds.

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Honda Thats engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt Honda Thats engines receive full disassembly with upgraded components addressing known EN07 failure points, while reconditioned units replace only worn parts; rebuilt offers better long-term value for high-mileage JDM imports.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: full disassembly, upgraded tensioners/rings/seals
Reconditioned: partial rebuild replacing visibly worn parts
Rebuilt typically £200-£400 more but longer lifespan
Both carry 12-month warranty through EngineMarket
